query: Julia Letlow John Fleming Louisiana Senate runoff debate 2026 televised
answer:
## Research Report: 2026 Louisiana Senate Runoff — Julia Letlow vs. John Fleming

### Overview

The 2026 Louisiana Senate race has produced one of the most dramatic primary outcomes in recent U.S. political history. Incumbent Republican Senator **Bill Cassidy** was knocked out in the May 16, 2026 Republican primary — finishing third — setting up a runoff between **U.S. Rep. Julia Letlow** and **State Treasurer John Fleming** on **June 27, 2026**. This is the first Senate election under Louisiana's restored partisan primary system (replacing the "jungle primary" that was eliminated in January 2024).

---

### Primary Results (May 16, 2026)

| Candidate | Votes | Percentage |
|-----------|-------|------------|
| **Julia Letlow** | 179,876 | **44.8%** |
| **John Fleming** | 113,428 | **28.3%** |
| Bill Cassidy (incumbent) | 99,479 | 24.8% |
| Mark Spencer | 8,335 | 2.1% |

Letlow led by over 16 points but fell short of the 50%+ threshold needed to avoid a runoff.

---

### Debates

**Primary-Phase Debate (Completed):**
- **Date:** May 5, 2026
- **Host:** KPEL-FM (radio), broadcast on Facebook Live
- **Moderators:** Moon Griffon and Jeff Beimfohr
- **Participants:** Julia Letlow and John Fleming (Bill Cassidy was absent; Mark Spencer was not invited)
- **Coverage:** The Times-Picayune/New Orleans Advocate reported: *"Julia Letlow, John Fleming spar over Trump, attack ads in Louisiana Senate race"*

**Runoff Debates:**
As of May 18, 2026, no additional runoff-phase debates have been publicly announced. The runoff is scheduled for June 27, 2026, leaving time for potential debate scheduling in the coming weeks.

---

### Polling Data

**Head-to-Head Runoff Polls (Letlow vs. Fleming):**
- **Quantus Insights (May 6–7, 2026):** Letlow 45%, Fleming 40%
- **BDPC (April 28–30, 2026):** Letlow 42%, Fleming 31%

**Pre-Primary Multi-Candidate Polls:**
- **Quantus Insights (May 6–7):** Letlow 42%, Fleming 30%, Cassidy 20%
- **Fabrizio, Lee & Associates (May 4–5):** Letlow 32%, Cassidy 26%, Fleming 21%
- **Emerson College (April 24–26):** Fleming 28%, Letlow 27%, Cassidy 21%

---

### Candidate Profiles

**Julia Letlow (R):**
- U.S. Representative, Louisiana's 5th Congressional District (since 2021)
- First Republican woman elected to Congress from Louisiana
- Won 2021 special election after husband Luke Letlow died from COVID-19 before taking office
- Ph.D. in communications; born 1981
- Received **Donald Trump's endorsement on January 17, 2026**
- Authored the Parents Bill of Rights Act

**John Fleming (R):**
- Louisiana State Treasurer (since 2024)
- Former U.S. Representative, LA-4 (2009–2017)
- Physician and Navy veteran; born 1951
- Served in Trump administration (Deputy Asst. Secretary for Health IT, Asst. Secretary of Commerce, White House aide)
- Previously ran for Senate in 2016 (finished 5th)

---

### Fundraising (as of May 17, 2026)

| Candidate | Raised | Spent | Cash on Hand |
|-----------|--------|-------|--------------|
| John Fleming | $11,286,639 | $9,897,447 | $1,389,264 |
| Julia Letlow | $4,398,631 | $2,785,529 | $1,613,102 |

Fleming outraised Letlow nearly 3-to-1 but spent heavily; Letlow holds a slight cash-on-hand advantage entering the runoff.

---

### Key Dates

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| Jan 17, 2026 | Trump endorses Letlow |
| May 5, 2026 | Primary debate on KPEL-FM |
| May 16, 2026 | Republican primary (Letlow wins plurality) |
| **June 27, 2026** | **Republican runoff (upcoming)** |
| Nov 3, 2026 | General election |

---

### Context & Significance

- **Cassidy's defeat** is the first time an elected incumbent senator lost renomination since Richard Lugar in 2012, driven largely by his vote to convict Trump in the second impeachment trial.
- **Campaign controversy:** Fleming accused the Letlow campaign of offering him a CDC position to drop out; Letlow's campaign denied the allegation.
- **General election outlook:** Louisiana is heavily Republican; the runoff winner will be the strong favorite in November against the Democratic nominee.

---

*Sources: Wikipedia articles on the 2026 United States Senate election in Louisiana, Julia Letlow, and John Fleming, accessed May 18, 2026.*

key_facts: Julia Letlow and John Fleming advanced to a Republican runoff for the Louisiana Senate seat on June 27, 2026, after no candidate won a majority in the May 16 primary.
Letlow won the primary with 44.8% (179,876 votes) vs. Fleming's 28.3% (113,428 votes); incumbent Bill Cassidy finished third with 24.8%.
One debate occurred during the primary phase on May 5, 2026, hosted by KPEL-FM radio (Moon Griffon and Jeff Beimfohr moderating), broadcast on Facebook Live. Cassidy was absent.
As of May 18, 2026, no runoff-phase televised or radio debates have been publicly announced for the June 27 runoff.
Letlow holds Trump's endorsement (received January 17, 2026), which was a decisive factor in Cassidy's third-place finish.
Head-to-head polling favors Letlow: Quantus Insights (May 6-7) shows Letlow 45% vs Fleming 40%; BDPC (April 28-30) shows Letlow 42% vs Fleming 31%.
Fleming outraised Letlow ($11.3M vs $4.4M) but spent more; Letlow has a slight cash-on-hand advantage ($1.61M vs $1.39M) entering the runoff.
Cassidy's loss marks the first time an elected incumbent senator lost renomination since Richard Lugar in 2012.
This is the first Louisiana Senate election under the restored partisan primary system (replacing the jungle primary eliminated in January 2024).
Fleming accused the Letlow campaign of offering him a CDC position to drop out; Letlow's campaign denied the allegation.
